data.title: Microsoft Releases AI Models for Transcription, Voice and Image Generation
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan 




  Microsoft unveiled three new artificial intelligence models offering speech-to-text transcription as well as voice and image generation. 



  The software giant said Thursday it's working to deploy the models to power its consumer and commercial products, and they are now available for its Foundry customers. 



  One of the new models, MAI-Transcribe-1, offers speech-to-text transcription across 25 languages. The model transcribes more than two times faster than Microsoft's existing Azure Fast offering, the company said. 



  The company's MAI-Voice-1 offering, meanwhile, aims to generate natural, realistic speech. Foundry users will also be able to create their own custom voice using a few seconds of audio. 



  Microsoft's image generation model, MAI-Image-2, is already in use across some enterprise partners including marketing and communications firm WPP, Microsoft said. The model allows users to generate images quickly with natural lighting, accurate skin tones and textures, the company said. 



  Microsoft has faced stumbling blocks in the race for dominance in AI. The company's Copilot chatbot, a product central to its AI strategy, hasn't won over users as a clear ChaptGPT alternative, and Wall Street has grown concerned that growth in its most important business unit, the Azure cloud-computing business, is slowing. Microsoft continues to double down on its AI efforts, with plans to invest billions globally in AI computing as demand booms. 





  Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  April 02, 2026 12:50 ET (16:50 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: MAPEI’s Luigi Di Geso Receives NTCA’s 2026 Joe A. Tarver Award at Coverings
data.description:
MAPEI’s Luigi Di Geso Receives NTCA’s 2026 Joe A. Tarver Award at Coverings

Recognition followed Gary Sinise keynote address sponsored by MAPEI Corporation

MAPEI Corporation announced today that Luigi Di Geso, President and CEO of MAPEI North America, has been awarded the National Tile Contractors Association’s (NTCA) prestigious Joe A. Tarver Service to the Industry Award for 2026. Di Geso was formally honored during Coverings 2026, following the Gary Sinise keynote address – an event that was proudly sponsored by MAPEI.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260402359033/en/

MAPEI North America's President and CEO Luigi Di Geso receives the Joe A. Tarver Award from NTCA's Executive Director Bart Bettiga at Coverings 2026.

Presented annually to one U.S.-based industry professional, the Joe A. Tarver Award recognizes individuals whose careers reflect extraordinary service, leadership and long-term impact on the tile and stone industry. Named after NTCA Executive Director Emeritus Joe Tarver, the award honors those who have strengthened the industry through advocacy, education and collaboration.

In announcing the award, Bart Bettiga, Executive Director of NTCA, praised Di Geso’s decades of commitment to the trade, stating: "The Joe Tarver Award is given to an individual who positively impacts the tile industry throughout their career. As the leader of MAPEI North America, Luigi Di Geso has left an indelible footprint on the tile and stone trade. MAPEI supports the NTCA and the entire industry in our effort to grow the tile market. MAPEI is a leader in training and education and through sponsorship and association support, they play a leading role in the proper specification of tile-installation materials, resulting in successful installations. Luigi and the entire team at MAPEI are deeply appreciated by the members and leaders of the National Tile Contractors Association and we are honored to recognize his accomplishments."

"I am deeply honored to receive this recognition from NTCA,” Di Geso said. “I love this industry and the people who make it what it is. My goal has always been to strengthen professionalism, education and collaboration among manufacturers, associations, distributors and contractors. When individuals grow, the entire industry grows – and that has guided my career every day."

Di Geso has been a member of the MAPEI family for more than two decades. From 2004 to 2009, he served as General Manager of MAPEI’s Canadian subsidiary before being appointed President and CEO of MAPEI North America. Under his leadership, the company has expanded from three product lines to 19, while increasing its operational footprint from 12 to 28 facilities across North America and the Caribbean.

During Di Geso’s tenure, MAPEI North America’s revenue has grown from $260 million to more than $1 billion annually, reflecting a strategy centered on innovation, sustainability and long-term investment. Di Geso has championed the development of environmentally responsible technologies that support circular construction practices and low-impact manufacturing.

Beyond MAPEI, Di Geso remains deeply engaged in advancing the broader construction industry, collaborating with more than 80 professional organizations that include the Tile Council of North America (TCNA), Greater Miami Chamber of Commerce, Italy-America Chamber of Commerce Southeast, Italian Chamber of Commerce in Canada and the Italian-Canadian Community Foundation.

Di Geso’s leadership emphasizes cross-functional excellence, integrating research and development, marketing, sales, customer service and technical services to deliver solutions that contractors can trust – reinforcing MAPEI’s reputation as a long-term partner for professionals across the building sector.

Di Geso received the Joe A. Tarver Award during the NTCA/TCNA Awards Program at Coverings 2026, which was held on Wednesday, April 1, 2026.

About MAPEI

Founded in 1937 in Milan, MAPEI is one of the world’s leading manufacturers of chemical products for the building industry and has contributed to the construction of some of the most important architectural and infrastructure works worldwide. With 98 subsidiaries operating in 59 countries and 106 manufacturing plants in 42 nations, MAPEI Group employs about 13,200 staff worldwide. The foundations for the success of the company are specialization, internationalization, research & development, and sustainability.

Headquartered in Deerfield Beach (Florida), MAPEI North America consists of the subsidiaries MAPEI Corporation (for the USA), MAPEI Inc. (for Canada) and MAPEI Caribe (for Puerto Rico and other Caribbean islands), whose 28 facilities collectively have a workforce of about 2,000 employees. MAPEI facilities are certified to be in conformance with requirements in the ISO 9001, ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 Standards for Management Systems. An environmentally conscious manufacturer that reinvests a percentage of its revenue into R&D, MAPEI offers training for architects, contractors, installers and distributors through the MAPEI Technical Institute. For more about MAPEI, visit www.mapei.com or call 1-800-42-MAPEI (1-800-426-2734).

MAPEI NORTH AMERICA – PRESS AND PUBLIC RELATIONS OFFICE Tel. 954-246-8793 – jkramer@mapei.com – www.mapei.com

data.title: Linde to Build New Air Separation Plant in North Carolina
data.description:
Linde to Build New Air Separation Plant in North Carolina

Linde announced today that it will build, own and operate a new facility for the supply of industrial gases in Garysburg, North Carolina.

The new air separation unit (ASU) will produce liquid oxygen, nitrogen, and argon, serving customers in the region. Acquisition of land and equipment for the new site has begun and the plant is expected to start up at the end of 2028.

“We are experiencing incredible growth across multiple end markets in the area,” said Todd Lawson, Vice President East Region, Linde. “This investment will add to our already robust production and supply network, to meet the current and future industrial gas needs of customers in the region.”

About Linde

Linde is a leading global industrial gases and engineering company with 2025 sales of $34 billion. We live our mission of making our world more productive every day by providing high-quality solutions, technologies and services which are making our customers more successful and helping to sustain, decarbonize and protect our planet.

Linde serves a variety of end markets such as chemicals & energy, food & beverage, electronics, healthcare, manufacturing, metals and mining. Linde’s industrial gases and technologies are used in countless applications, enabling space exploration and launch technologies, delivering ultra-high-purity and specialty gases for semiconductor manufacturing, providing life-saving medical oxygen and enabling clean hydrogen production and carbon capture to reduce greenhouse gas emissions. Linde also delivers state-of-the-art gas processing solutions to support customer growth, efficiency improvements and emissions reductions.

For more information about the company and its products and services, please visit www.linde.com

Media Relations
Leslie Agee
Phone : 918.960.1721
Email : leslie.agee@linde.com

data.title: How Rental Expert Daniel Tiser of Plymouth Explains Tent Rental Preparation for HelloNation
data.description:
How Rental Expert Daniel Tiser of Plymouth Explains Tent Rental Preparation for HelloNation

How Rental Expert Daniel Tiser of Plymouth Explains Tent Rental Preparation for HelloNation

PR Newswire

PLYMOUTH, Ind., April 2, 2026

PLYMOUTH, Ind., April 2, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- What should you know before a tent rental in Plymouth? A HelloNation article provides a clear look at how local weather, site conditions, and regional rules shape the tent rental process for events throughout northern Indiana. The piece, written for readers across the Michiana region, highlights how early planning, communication with vendors, and awareness of policies can make all the difference for a successful event.

In the feature, Daniel Tiser, a Rental Expert based in Plymouth and owner of Michiana Rental, helps readers understand why preparation is the single most important factor in tent rental success. According to the HelloNation article, renters who account for weather changes and site challenges avoid the kinds of last-minute problems that often cause stress during setup. The article explains that Plymouth sits in a part of Indiana where weather conditions can change quickly, meaning rental companies often adjust schedules to meet narrow delivery windows.

The tent rental process, as Tiser describes it, begins long before the tent is raised. Many clients start with tent size, but he notes that the condition of the ground is just as important. Different surfaces—grass, gravel, or pavement—require different staking or ballasting methods, which can alter both cost and setup time. The HelloNation article points out that local companies often request photos or even site visits before confirming a booking to check for underground utilities or soft areas that could make installation unsafe.

For residents across the Michiana region, these steps may feel tedious, but they prevent bigger issues later. In Plymouth, unpredictable rainfall and wind make it essential to plan for solid footing and quick drainage around tents. Daniel Tiser explains that understanding these local conditions helps clients avoid last-minute delays that can affect an entire event schedule. By focusing on preparation, renters not only protect their investment but also ensure a smoother experience for everyone involved.

Tent rental in the Michiana region also involves understanding local permit requirements. The HelloNation article notes that larger tents, especially those placed near public spaces or sidewalks, may need special approvals from the city. These permits are often tied to fire safety codes, requiring specific exit layouts or restrictions on sidewalls. Clients who wait too long to apply may face rushed timelines or unexpected setup limits. Tiser's advice is to check with local authorities well before the event date to prevent installation delays.

Hidden costs also play a major role in the rental experience. The article explains that delivery fees, cleaning charges, and fuel surcharges often appear in contracts but are easy to overlook. During peak months, when crews travel across county lines, these fees can add up quickly. Plymouth's narrow access routes and soft lawns can further complicate delivery, leading to additional charges for labor or equipment. The HelloNation piece emphasizes reading every line of the rental contract carefully to identify such costs early.

Another key point from the article is how weather impacts cancellation and rescheduling terms. In the Michiana region, where storms can arrive without warning, some companies offer partial refunds or credit toward future rentals, while others limit flexibility once crews are en route. Tiser underscores the value of reviewing these policies in advance to avoid disappointment when severe weather makes setup unsafe. By planning around local weather patterns, clients can make informed decisions about timing, protection, and event layout.

Beyond tent rental, the HelloNation article extends its insights to tool and equipment rental across Plymouth and surrounding towns. Homeowners renting generators, lifts, or compactors are advised to review fuel and cleaning rules, as these often influence return conditions and fees. Many rental companies, including Michiana Rental, provide short safety demonstrations before each rental. Tiser encourages renters to ask questions about proper operation and storage to avoid damage or additional charges.

Through examples and practical advice, the article reinforces that communication is the key to a successful rental experience. Whether the project involves an outdoor wedding, a community event, or a home repair job, having a clear checklist—covering site readiness, permits, weather planning, and contract terms—helps prevent confusion and delay. As Tiser notes, when clients approach tent rental with clear expectations, both the renter and provider benefit from a smoother, more predictable process.

In Plymouth and throughout the Michiana region, tent rental is not just about picking the right tent—it's about preparation, coordination, and understanding how local conditions influence every step. By applying the guidance shared in the HelloNation article, renters can approach their next event with greater confidence and fewer surprises.

What Should You Know Before a Tent Rental in Plymouth features insights from Daniel Tiser, Rental Expert of Plymouth, Indiana, in HelloNation.

About HelloNation
HelloNation is a premier media platform that connects readers with trusted professionals and businesses across various industries. Through its innovative "edvertising" approach that blends educational content and storytelling, HelloNation delivers expert-driven articles that inform, inspire, and empower. Covering topics from home improvement and health to business strategy and lifestyle, HelloNation highlights leaders making a meaningful impact in their communities.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/how-rental-expert-daniel-tiser-of-plymouth-explains-tent-rental-preparation-for-hellonation-302733072.html

SOURCE HelloNation

data.title: KBRA Assigns Preliminary Ratings to ClearLight Nebo Issuer 2026-1, LLC
data.description:
KBRA Assigns Preliminary Ratings to ClearLight Nebo Issuer 2026-1, LLC

KBRA assigns preliminary ratings to two classes of notes issued by ClearLight Nebo Issuer 2026-1, LLC. The transaction is collateralized by a diversified pool of 15,555 leases and power purchase agreements (PPAs) associated with residential solar photovoltaic installations (PV Systems). The total Aggregate Discounted Solar Asset Balance (ADSAB) based on a discount rate of 7.5%, consisting of the discounted payments of the leases and PPAs is approximately $386.1 million.

The three largest geographic concentrations include California, Florida, and Texas, which together represent approximately 74.8% of the number of PV Systems and approximately 80.8% of the ADSAB. The portfolio consists of 79.4% PPAs (all of which are fixed) and 20.6% lease agreements by ADSAB of customer contracts with monthly payments. The weighted average original and remaining tenor of the PPAs and leases is 300 months and 295 months, respectively. The weighted average FICO of the underlying customers of the PV Systems is 762.

To access ratings and relevant documents, click here.

Click here to view the report.

Methodologies

  • ABS: General Global Rating Methodology for Asset Backed Securities
  • Structured Finance: Global Structured Finance Counterparty Methodology
  • ESG Global Rating Methodology

Disclosures

Further information on key credit considerations, sensitivity analyses that consider what factors can affect these credit ratings and how they could lead to an upgrade or a downgrade, and ESG factors (where they are a key driver behind the change to the credit rating or rating outlook) can be found in the full rating report referenced above.

A description of all substantially material sources that were used to prepare the credit rating and information on the methodology(ies) (inclusive of any material models and sensitivity analyses of the relevant key rating assumptions, as applicable) used in determining the credit rating is available in the Information Disclosure Form(s) located here.

Information on the meaning of each rating category can be located here.

Further disclosures relating to this rating action are available in the Information Disclosure Form(s) referenced above. Additional information regarding KBRA policies, methodologies, rating scales and disclosures are available at www.kbra.com.

About KBRA

Kroll Bond Rating Agency, LLC (KBRA), one of the major credit rating agencies (CRA), is a full-service CRA registered with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission as an NRSRO. Kroll Bond Rating Agency Europe Limited is registered as a CRA with the European Securities and Markets Authority. Kroll Bond Rating Agency UK Limited is registered as a CRA with the UK Financial Conduct Authority. In addition, KBRA is designated as a Designated Rating Organization (DRO) by the Ontario Securities Commission for issuers of asset-backed securities to file a short form prospectus or shelf prospectus. KBRA is also recognized as a Qualified Rating Agency by Taiwan’s Financial Supervisory Commission and is recognized by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners as a Credit Rating Provider (CRP) in the U.S.

data.title: The S&P 500 could hit bottom by May - and 6,000 is the stock market's correction floor
data.description:
By Mark Hulbert

Strong one-day rallies are far more common in weak markets

After the stock market's huge rally on March 31, plus another up day on April 1, discussing a correction might seem overly pessimistic. But in fact, strong single-day rallies like the one earlier this week are far more common in weak markets.

If the stock market's recent decline turns out to be no worse than a garden-variety correction, the S&P 500 SPX will hit bottom on May 21 around the 6,000 level.

That's assuming the length and depth of any correction will match the average of all S&P 500 corrections since 1928. On that assumption, after hitting its late May bottom, the stock market will rebound - and by Oct. 12, it should surpass its Jan. 27, 2026, all-time closing high.

These specific timelines are illustrated in the chart below, which reflects the Nasdaq Composite COMP since its creation in 1971. The red line - at the 24.8% mark - shows the percentage of days since then that occurred during one of the major bear markets in the bull/bear calendar maintained by Ned Davis Research. Notice that a far higher percentage of big one-day rallies occurred during one of those bear markets - in the 60% to 80% range.

The image shows a chart indicating that, historically, a significant percentage of daily market rallies occur during bear markets.

Your perspective in this discussion hinges on whether you're a long-term investor or short-term trader. For long-term investors, corrections are nothing more than a cost of doing business. Having to wait six months until the market is back at all-time-high territory (which will be the case if the correction is average) is a small price to fully participate in the stock market's long-term uptrend.

Short-term traders will lament that, even if this correction is no worse than average, the stock market will shed 10% and you'll have to wait until October to be made whole again.

Regardless of your perspective, you should know that there is significant variation in the sample on either side of this historical average. As you can see below, S&P 500 corrections over the past century have been as short as 13 days and as long as 531 days (18 months). Subsequent recoveries to new all-time highs have been as quick as 22 days and as slow as 589 days (19 months).

This discussion so far has assumed that U.S. market weakness will bring no worse than a typical correction - a decline of 10% to 20%.

If it is worse and this is actually the start of a bear market - and its length and loss are equal to the average over the past century - the S&P 500 will bottom nine months from now, on Jan. 2, 2027, at 4,429, for a loss of about 30%. And the market's subsequent recovery won't surpass the Jan. 27, 2026 record high until April 2, 2031.

That is definitely a scarier prospect. Fortunately, bear markets are less frequent than corrections. Of the greater-than-10% declines in the S&P 500 since 1928, 39% represented the start of a bear market. So there may be some solace in knowing that, based on these numbers alone, a bear market probably isn't beginning.

Mark Hulbert is a regular contributor to MarketWatch. His Hulbert Ratings tracks investment newsletters that pay a flat fee to be audited. He can be reached at mark@hulbertratings.com

More: Why investors shouldn't get too excited about this week's stock-market rebound

Plus: 'Liberation day' one year later: What Trump's tariffs are costing America

-Mark Hulbert

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  04-02-26 1219ET


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Haig Partners Serves as Exclusive Advisor on the Divestiture of Hello Auto Group’s Valencia Platform
data.description:
Haig Partners Serves as Exclusive Advisor on the Divestiture of Hello Auto Group’s Valencia Platform

Transactions Include the Sale of Hello Subaru of Valencia, Hello Kia of Valencia, and Hello Mazda of Valencia in the Valencia Auto Mall

Haig Partners LLC, a leading buy-sell advisory firm to auto retailers in the United States, served as the exclusive sell-side advisor to Hello Auto Group on the sale of its Santa Clarita platform located in the Valencia Auto Mall, consisting of Hello Subaru of Valencia, Hello Kia of Valencia, and Hello Mazda of Valencia.

The transactions represent a strategic repositioning by Hello Auto Group as it continues to optimize its portfolio and focus on long-term growth initiatives. Haig Partners advised the company and its Board of Directors throughout a coordinated, multi-transaction process.

Hello Subaru of Valencia was acquired by Jerry Seiner Dealerships, an established and expanding dealership group with operations across the Western United States. Hello Mazda of Valencia and Hello Kia of Valencia were acquired by Ghreiwati Auto Group, a diversified operator with an established presence in Southern California.

California continues to attract significant interest from sophisticated buyers, both domestic and international. Demand has been supported by improving regulatory clarity, including developments related to CARB, as well as the state’s position as the largest automotive market in the United States and one of the largest economies globally. Continued economic strength, supported in part by growth in AI and technology, has reinforced buyer confidence and transaction activity. Buyers who have invested in California in prior cycles are benefiting today, and sellers entering the market are well positioned to achieve strong outcomes.

“It was a privilege to represent Hello Auto Group and its Board in this strategic divestiture,” said Jayson Crouch, Managing Director at Haig Partners. “We brought the assets to market during a dynamic period for buy-sell activity in California, and as clarity improved around CARB and broader industry factors, including tariffs, buyer demand and valuations strengthened meaningfully.

We ran a disciplined, competitive process with a broad group of sophisticated buyers across the country. By maintaining control of the process and aligning timing with market conditions, we maximized competitive tension and delivered an outcome that reflected the full value of the assets and exceeded our client’s expectations.”

“Alan Haig, Jayson Crouch and the Haig Partners team did a fantastic job representing the interests of all board members and securing an excellent outcome,” said Paul Walser, Partner and Board Member of Hello Auto Group and Partner of Walser Automotive Group. “They ran a confidential and disciplined process, executed every step with professionalism and maintained alignment across a complex group of stakeholders. They never overpromised but delivered a result that reflected the strength of the business while maintaining transparency and integrity throughout.”

“Alan Haig and Jayson Crouch ran a complete process for us as we divested the Santa Clarita platform and repositioned the company for the future,” said Pat Denzer, Partner and Board Member of Hello Auto Group. “They were trusted advisors who kept us informed throughout the process and ultimately delivered an outcome that exceeded shareholder expectations.”

“I would like to thank Jayson Crouch, Alan Haig, and the entire team at Haig Partners for their hard work and professionalism in completing the transactions involving our stores in Valencia, California,” said Karl Schmidt, President of Hello Auto Group. “The process was completed in three segments with two buyers as part of the divestiture of our Valencia platform. The team ran a thorough and disciplined process that generated strong interest and delivered an outcome that exceeded our expectations. They were professional, detailed, responsive, and respectful of all parties’ interests throughout.”

Dealers interested in learning more about buying or selling a dealership in California should contact Jayson Crouch, Managing Director at Haig Partners, at Jayson@HaigPartners.com.

About Haig Partners

Haig Partners is a leading buy-sell advisory firm that helps owners of higher-value dealerships maximize the value of their businesses when they are ready to sell. The team at Haig Partners has advised on the purchase or sale of more than 580 dealerships and has represented 32 dealership groups that qualify for the Top 150 Dealership Groups list published by Automotive News, more than any other firm.

Clients of Haig Partners benefit from the group’s collective experience as former executives with leading companies such as AutoNation, Bank of America, Credit Suisse, Deloitte, FORVIS, J.P. Morgan, Lexus, Porsche, and Toyota Financial Services. Leveraging its unmatched expertise and extensive relationships, Haig Partners guides clients to successful outcomes through a confidential and customized sales process.

The firm authors The Haig Report®, the leading quarterly report tracking trends in auto retail and their impact on dealership values, and co-authors NADA’s Guide, Buying and Selling a Dealership. Haig Partners team members are frequent speakers at industry conferences and are regularly quoted in Reuters, Forbes, The Wall Street Journal, The New York Times, CNBC, BBC, Automotive News, Wards, and CarDealershipGuy. For more information, https://www.haigpartners.com.

data.title: Mortgage rates rise for the fifth week in a row. Here's how much more homebuyers are paying since the Iran war's start.
data.description:
Andrew Keshner

One year ago, the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage averaged 6.64%

Home buyers have encountered higher borrowing costs in recent weeks.

Mortgage rates have continued to climb as the war in Iran drags on, increasing for the fifth consecutive week and reaching their highest point since early September.

The average 30-year fixed-rate mortgage reached 6.46%, up from 6.38% a week ago, Freddie Mac (FMCC) said Thursday. However, that's still below the 6.64% level where it was at this time last year.

Mortgage rates tend to follow the yield on the 10-year Treasury note BX:TMUBMUSD10Y, which was lower on Thursday, at 4.304%, at last check.

The new mortgage-rate data come on the heels of President Donald Trump's national address Wednesday night, in which he emphasized the U.S. military's accomplishments against the Iranian regime. Operation Epic Fury's "core strategic objectives are nearing completion," Trump said. Stock markets fell in response, after climbing in recent days on hopes for an end in the fighting.

Gas prices will fall quickly after the war ends, Trump said. Since the conflict was launched by the U.S. and Israel on Feb. 28, prices at the pump have jumped by $1 a gallon, mirroring the swift rise in mortgage costs potential home buyers are seeing. Some simple math shows the price of the recent rate run-up.

Just before the start of fighting in the Middle East, mortgage rates had dropped to their lowest level since 2022, with the 30-year rate at 5.98% on Feb. 26, Freddie Mac data show.

Someone buying a home this week for $398,000 - the median sales price of an existing home in February, according to the National Association of Realtors - with a 20% down payment and a 6.46% mortgage rate would pay an estimated $2,383 per month, according to a Zillow (Z) mortgage calculator. (The calculation includes an estimate of monthly expenses for taxes and insurance.)

That's essentially $100 more per month than they would have paid if the same sale had occurred in late February at a 5.98% mortgage rate, the Zillow calculator shows.

Over the life of the loan, that's approximately $36,000 more paid toward interest, the calculator shows.

Last month, the median monthly mortgage payment was $2,742, according to Redfin (RKT). It was a small year-over-year increase of 0.3%, but it was the first increase since October, the analysis showed.

There's been a large run-up in rates in a short period, but that's not historically unprecedented, according to Jeff DerGurahian, chief investment officer and head economist at loanDepot (LDI).

Borrowing costs are a major factor in a home buyer's decision, but they are not the only factor. It's too soon to say how the mortgage-rate increases will affect the spring home-buying season, he noted.

The March jobs report, due out Friday, "has the potential to matter more than most of the data releases we've seen since this conflict began," he said.

Also read: The U.S. isn't creating many jobs anymore. The March jobs report won't buck the trend.

A below-forecast jobs number would put more focus on the economy's "underlying softness" and "help improve the outlook for rates, although mortgage rates may not improve as quickly as Treasury yields," DerGurahian said. But an above-forecast number "could reinforce the idea that borrowing costs may stay elevated for longer."

Either way, DerGurahian said, "bringing mortgage rates meaningfully back toward 6% is unlikely to happen overnight. It will likely take more than one report, along with some easing in the Middle East, before we see a more durable move lower."

-Andrew Keshner
